event: transported to our hospital by ambulance
hemorrhage in bilateral lateral ventricles with left dominance
hemorrhage was confined within the ventricles
source of bleeding was unclear in the arterial phase
spot enhancement on the lateral wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
on the third day of admission, cerebral angiograph
revealed an aneurysm (3.8 mm) at the distal site of the mLSA arising from the left ACA
considered to be a ruptured aneurysm
to prevent re-bleeding, aneurysm embolization was performed
guiding sheath was navigated into the petrous portion of the left internal carotid artery
Marathon catheter was advanced to the left A1
selective angiography from the DeFrictor confirmed the catheter tip location
slight reflux of NBCA was observed in the proximal artery
no signs of hemorrhagic complications on postoperative head CT
no cerebral infarction on postoperative head CT
neither impaired consciousness nor paralysis developed
Lipiodol remained as a high-density spot
a decrease in muscle strength associated with disuse remained
